mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month change in FEDFUNDS raises equity discount rates and tightens financial conditions, reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when the signal is evaluated continuously.
failure_condition: Reject if the 3-month change in FEDFUNDS has a nonnegative predictive slope for 20-day S&P 500 returns over three consecutive walk-forward years or Rank IC is below 0.03 in absolute value.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation remains elevated while FEDFUNDS is rising, markets price additional tightening and lower valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth crosses above 6% while FEDFUNDS 3-month change is positive
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if the interaction term between CPIAUCSL growth and FEDFUNDS change is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A very low UNRATE combined with rising PAYEMS signals labor-market overheating, increasing expected wage pressure and policy tightening, which lowers S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses below 3.7% and PAYEMS is above its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-UNRATE and rising-PAYEMS condition is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in RSAFS signals resilient household demand and near-term revenue support for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS momentum does not produce higher average 20-day S&P 500 returns than lower momentum states in walk-forward tests, with t-test p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST signals contraction in the rate-sensitive housing channel, weakening future construction activity and credit demand, which reduces S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST contractions are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or the conditional mean return is not below baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in INDPRO signals improving industrial output and operating leverage, increasing expected earnings and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO 3-month momentum has a nonpositive coefficient for 60-day S&P 500 returns across two consecutive walk-forward windows or Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL together with falling INDPRO identifies a stagflation shock: margins compress while discount rates rise, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month change is positive and INDPRO 3-month change crosses below 0%
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL-up and INDPRO-down interaction is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or the interaction coefficient is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in UNRATE signals deterioration in labor income and recession risk, reducing expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month change crosses above 0.3 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are not followed by below-baseline 60-day S&P 500 returns or if positive returns occur in more than 60% of triggered cases.
